scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT WITH COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court.

PROCEDURAL COMPLIANCE

4. The Plaintiff states that the present suit is a commercial dispute within the meaning of Section 2(1)(c) of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015. The Plaintiff has complied with the mandatory pre-institution mediation under Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, [OR: The Plaintiff claims exemption from pre-institution mediation under Section 12A due to urgency / interim relief requirement].

LIMITATION

5. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from date fixed for performance, or if no date fixed, when plaintiff has notice that performance is refused. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

6. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into an Agreement to Sell in respect of the residential property bearing Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, for a total consideration of Rs. 85,00,000/-. The said Agreement to Sell was executed {{AGREEMENT_REGISTRATION_DETAILS}} and is marked as Annexure A.

7. In terms of the said Agreement, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- to the Defendant on 10.01.2024 via RTGS bearing UTR No. HDFC20240110987654, the receipt of which is marked as Annexure B. The balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- was agreed to be paid on or before 10.07.2024 against the execution and registration of the Sale Deed.

8. The Plaintiff has always been ready and willing to perform her part of the obligations under the Agreement to Sell, including the payment of the balance consideration within the stipulated time, and continues to remain so ready and willing to date.

BREACH OF CONTRACT

9. Despite the Plaintiff's readiness and willingness, the Defendant vide letter dated 05.06.2024 expressly refused to execute the Sale Deed, stating that he had received a higher offer for the suit property. The said refusal letter is marked as Annexure D.

10. Aggrieved by the Defendant's refusal, the Plaintiff issued a Legal Notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to execute the Sale Deed in terms of the Agreement. The copy of the Legal Notice along with the postal receipt is marked as Annexure C.

11. The Defendant has failed and refused to comply with the contents of the Legal Notice and has not executed the Sale Deed, thereby committing a clear breach of the contractual obligations stipulated in the Agreement to Sell.

DAMAGES

12. By reason of the Defendant's wrongful refusal and breach of contract, the Plaintiff has suffered significant prejudice and financial loss, having locked funds worth Rs. 25,00,000/- and being deprived of the use of the property for which the Agreement was entered.

13. The Plaintiff is therefore entitled to recover the costs of this suit and any further compensation as may be deemed fit by this Court for the delay and harassment caused by the Defendant's unjustified refusal to perform the contract.

LEGAL BASIS

14. The Plaintiff is entitled to a decree of specific performance of the agreement under Sections 10 and 14 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, the subject matter being such that the grant of compensation in money would not afford adequate relief.

15. The Plaintiff has been ready and willing to perform her part of the agreement at all material times within the meaning of Section 16(c) of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.

16. The present suit is maintainable under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

CAUSE OF ACTION

17. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

18. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated 20.06.2024, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

19. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

20. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Appropriate court fee has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Court Fees Act, 1870 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

21. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this suit till the date of judgment / decree, under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as this Court deems fit and just from the date of judgment / decree till the date of realisation of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree of specific performance directing the Defendant to perform and complete the agreement dated 10.01.2024 in favour of the Plaintiff;
    (b) Direct the Defendant to execute and register the necessary conveyance / sale deed in favour of the Plaintiff upon receipt of the balance consideration;
    (c) In the alternative, award compensation / damages in lieu of specific performance;
    (d) Award costs of the suit;
    (e) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Original agreement dated 10.01.2024

Annexure B — RTGS receipt (UTR: HDFC20240110987654)

Annexure C — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt

Annexure D — Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024

VERIFICATION

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 21 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

DEPONENT

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

PLAINTIFF

                                    THROUGH:
                                    {{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
                                    Advocate for the Plaintiff
                                    Enrollment No. {{ENROLLMENT_NO}}
                                    {{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
                                    Mobile: {{ADVOCATE_PHONE}}
                                    Email: {{ADVOCATE_EMAIL}}

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE COMMERCIAL COURT (COMMERCIAL DIVISION)
AT BENGALURU

Commercial Suit No. __________ / 20____

TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd,
A company registered under the Companies Act,
Having its registered office at: MG Road, Bengaluru.
Represented by its Director, Priya Menon, aged 40 years.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

CloudBridge Systems LLP,
A Limited Liability Partnership having its office at:
Whitefield, Bengaluru.
Represented by its Designated Partner, Vikram Singh, aged 48 years.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

COMMERCIAL COURT MAINTAINABILITY

1. The subject matter of the present suit arises out of a commercial dispute within the meaning of Section 2(1)(c) of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

2. The suit is valued above the specified value of {{SPECIFIED_VALUE_THRESHOLD}} as prescribed under Section 12 of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, and is therefore maintainable before this Hon'ble Commercial Court.

JURISDICTION

3. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

4. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

5. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court. This Hon'ble Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain commercial disputes under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

LIMITATION

6. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 55 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from when the contract is broken. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

SECTION 12A COMPLIANCE

7. The Plaintiff states that the mandatory pre-institution mediation under Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015 has been {{MEDIATION_COMPLIANCE_STATUS}}. {{MEDIATION_DETAILS_IF_APPLICABLE}}

FACTS OF THE CASE

8. The Plaintiff is a company registered under the Companies Act, having its registered office at MG Road, Bengaluru, and is represented in this matter by its Director, Priya Menon, aged 40 years.

9. The Defendant is a limited liability partnership having its office at Whitefield, Bengaluru, and is represented by its Designated Partner, Vikram Singh, aged 48 years.

10. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into a Software Development Agreement for the development of an ERP system for a total contract value of Rs. 45,00,000/-, marked as Annexure A.

11. Pursuant to the Agreement, the Plaintiff paid a sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- as the first milestone payment to the Defendant via NEFT on 01.02.2024, bearing UTR No. ICIC20240201456789, marked as Annexure B.

12. As per the terms of the Agreement, the Defendant was obligated to deliver the first module of the ERP system by 01.05.2024.

BREACH PARTICULARS

13. The Defendant failed to deliver the first module or any part of the ERP system by the agreed deadline of 01.05.2024, thereby breaching the terms recorded in Clause {{SPECIFIC_CLAUSE_BREACHED}} of the Agreement.

14. On 15.06.2024, the Defendant abandoned the project entirely without delivering any modules, as evidenced by the email correspondence marked as Annexure C.

15. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice to the Defendant dated 01.07.2024 calling upon the Defendant to refund the advance and compensate for losses, marked as Annexure E.

16. The Defendant {{LEGAL_NOTICE_RESPONSE_DETAILS}} and has failed to return the advance payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- despite the expiry of the notice period.

DAMAGES

17. Due to the non-delivery and abandonment of the project, the Plaintiff suffered additional loss of Rs. 12,00,000/- due to delay in operations, as assessed in the loss assessment report by the auditor marked as Annexure D, calculated based on {{CONSEQUENTIAL_DAMAGES_CALCULATION_METHOD}}.

18. The Plaintiff is entitled to recover the total sum of Rs. 32,00,000/-, comprising Rs. 20,00,000/- as refund of advance and Rs. 12,00,000/- as consequential damages.

19. The Plaintiff claims interest at the rate of 18% per annum on the outstanding amount from the date of breach until realisation.

LEGAL BASIS

20. The Defendant has committed a breach of the agreement by failing to perform the obligations undertaken therein, thereby entitling the Plaintiff to claim damages under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

21. The Defendant has committed a repudiatory breach by refusing to perform its obligations, entitling the Plaintiff to treat the contract as at an end and seek appropriate relief.

22. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which provides that when a contract has been broken, the party who suffers by such breach is entitled to receive compensation for any loss or damage caused thereby, which naturally arose in the usual course of things from such breach, or which the parties knew, when they made the contract, to be likely to result from the breach of it.

23. The present suit is maintainable under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

CAUSE OF ACTION

24. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 15.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

25. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated 01.07.2024, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

26. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

27. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Appropriate court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Court Fees Act, 1870 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

28. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this suit till the date of judgment / decree, under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of judgment / decree till the date of realisation of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for a sum of Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) towards damages as particularised in the Schedule of Damages annexed hereto;
    (b) Award pendente lite and future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems just under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (c) Award costs of the suit including costs of the legal notice;
    (d) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

SCHEDULE OF DAMAGES / PARTICULARS OF DAMAGES

The Plaintiff claims the following heads of damages with computation methodology:

29. Actual Loss
   Amount claimed: Rs. {{ACTUAL_LOSS_AMOUNT}}/-
   Basis / methodology: {{ACTUAL_LOSS_BASIS}}
   Documentary proof: {{ACTUAL_LOSS_PROOF}}

30. Consequential los
